purpose: Guide Live News entertainment coverage, article-page quality, and social draft quality without copying publisher wording, public comments, or private user data.
nonNegotiables: Use this as pattern memory only; never copy another publisher's wording, headline structure, caption, comment, or creator voice.
Entertainment stories still require attribution, original source links, exact Live News story URLs for social posts, and human approval before publishing to social platforms.
Do not learn from private messages, personal profiles, usernames, copied comments, or individual identities.
Do not turn celebrity, health, death, legal, children, relationship, or allegation stories into gossip bait.
When facts are thin, stay narrow and source-backed instead of inventing context.

sourceMap.name: Entertainment Tonight
sourceMap.role: high-reach video-first celebrity and entertainment news model
sourceMap.patternLesson: Strong entertainment posts often combine a clear celebrity/personality hook with short video-friendly context and broad platform distribution.

sourceMap.name: E! News
sourceMap.patternLesson: Entertainment coverage benefits from immediacy, recognisable faces, event timing, and a conversational tone that still stays factual.

sourceMap.name: Variety
sourceMap.patternLesson: Industry-facing entertainment stories need business context: platform, studio, release, deal, audience, awards, or career stakes.

sourceMap.name: Billboard
sourceMap.patternLesson: Music coverage becomes stronger when it identifies the measurable fan signal: chart movement, release timing, tour momentum, streaming, radio, or industry dispute.

sourceMap.name: People
sourceMap.patternLesson: Personality-driven entertainment coverage should explain the human angle without invading privacy or overstating emotion.

sourceMap.name: Access Hollywood
sourceMap.patternLesson: Interview and event coverage works when it brings audiences behind the scene and makes the event, person, and moment easy to place.

sourceMap.name: TheWrap
sourceMap.role: entertainment business, media, and awards-season model
sourceMap.patternLesson: Entertainment business coverage should separate confirmed facts from analysis and explain why the move matters to the industry.

sourceMap.name: Meta Reels guidance
sourceMap.patternLesson: Entertainment social packaging should be visual-first, mobile-first, concise, and tested across variants instead of relying on one generic caption.

sourceMap.name: Pew Research social news patterns
sourceMap.patternLesson: Entertainment distribution should consider platform differences because younger audiences and platform-specific audiences discover news differently.

coveragePillars.label: Celebrity public moment
coveragePillars.readerQuestion: What happened with a public figure, and why are fans paying attention now?

coveragePillars.label: Music momentum
coveragePillars.readerQuestion: What changed for the artist, song, album, tour, chart, or fan base?

coveragePillars.label: Film, TV, and streaming
coveragePillars.readerQuestion: What changed for viewers: release, renewal, cancellation, casting, trailer, review wave, or platform move?

coveragePillars.label: Awards and red carpet
coveragePillars.readerQuestion: Who won, who appeared, what moment stood out, and what does it mean for the season?
avoid: low-value outfit-only posts when stronger news exists

coveragePillars.label: Entertainment business
coveragePillars.readerQuestion: What deal, dispute, ranking, strike, contract, studio decision, or streaming move affects the industry?
avoid: pretending business analysis is confirmed fact
missing who pays or who benefits

coveragePillars.label: Fan utility
coveragePillars.readerQuestion: What can a fan watch, stream, listen to, buy, attend, or follow next?
avoid: ticket links or purchase claims without source

coreSequence: Name the public figure, show, film, song, event, or company immediately.
State the concrete change in one sentence.
Add why it matters to fans, audiences, workers, the industry, or culture.
Include what happens next only if source-backed.
Point to the exact Live News page and original source attribution.

communicationPrinciples.lesson: Know whether the reader is a fan, casual viewer, industry follower, parent, music listener, or streaming subscriber before choosing the angle.
Entertainment writing can be warm and emotional, but credibility comes from attribution, timing, and avoiding rumor language.
Use a light version of attention, relevance, context, visualization, and action: hook the moment, explain why it matters, show the audience impact, then invite the exact article click.
Emotion should match the story: excitement for releases, reflection for career moments, care for deaths or health, restraint for allegations.
Mix short direct sentences with one context sentence. Avoid repeating the title and avoid same-tempo captions across nearby posts.

captionShapes.shape: Fan-facing hook -> source-backed context -> exact Live News page CTA
captionShapes.useWhen: The story affects fans directly through a release, tour, show, trailer, award, or public moment.

captionShapes.shape: Business move -> affected company/talent/audience -> why it matters
captionShapes.useWhen: The story is about studios, streaming, contracts, ratings, strikes, box office, or chart methodology.
The story is about a celebrity statement, life event, tribute, health update, or career milestone.

captionShapes.shape: What is new -> where/when audiences can follow it -> source attribution
captionShapes.useWhen: The story has practical fan utility such as release dates, platform availability, episode timing, or tour dates.

captionShapes.shape: Event result -> standout moment -> next awards/cultural context
captionShapes.useWhen: The story comes from awards shows, festivals, red carpets, finales, premieres, or live performances.

qualityChecklist: Does the post explain what happened without copying the source?
Does it identify the person, project, song, film, show, studio, platform, or event?
Does it avoid gossip when only verified public facts are available?
Does it include a human or fan angle without inventing public reaction?
Does it avoid hashtags unrelated to the article?
Does it point to an exact internal Live News story URL before public posting?

instagram.notes: Instagram drafts should feel like a visual entertainment card, not a database entry.

facebook.notes: Facebook drafts should explain why the story is worth clicking and make the exact article link obvious.

inputsAllowed: public trend tools without personal data
futureBuildSteps: Add Entertainment column card rules for celebrity, music, TV, film, awards, and fan utility.
Add category-specific headline and summary generation for entertainment.
Add generated Instagram card layouts for music, film/TV, celebrity, and awards.
Add performance lessons that compare entertainment caption shapes without storing private data.
